verses.text: Como as armações do Tabernáculo, o altar quadrado das ofertas queimadas foi feito de madeira de acácia revestida com metal. No entanto, em conformidade com as outras características fora do santuário, foi revestido com bronze ( **27.2-3** ; veja **27.10** , **17** , **19** ).\n\n

A arqueologia mostrou que os chifres do altar eram projeções que se erguiam dos cantos da estrutura. Talvez eles impedissem a madeira e as ofertas de caírem (veja **Sl 118.27** ). Eles também parecem ter tido um significado teológico, representando o cuidado gracioso de Deus. Uma pessoa que segurava os chifres do altar estava pedindo clemência no julgamento (veja **1Rs 1.50-51** ; **2.28** ). Os chifres eram ungidos com sangue em cerimônias de expiação e purificação ( **Êx 29.12** ; **Lv 4.7** ).

27:3 shovels—fire shovels for scraping together any of the scattered ashes. basons—for receiving the blood of the sacrifice to be sprinkled on the people. fleshhooks—curved, three-pronged forks (1 Sam 2:13-14). fire-pans—A large sort of vessel, wherein the sacred fire which came down from heaven (Lev 9:24) was kept burning, while they cleaned the altar and the grate from the coals and ashes, and while the altar was carried from one place to another in the wilderness [PATRICK, SPENCER, LE CLERC].
27:4 a grate of network of brass—sunk latticework to support the fire. four brazen rings—by which the grating might be lifted and taken away as occasion required from the body of the altar.
O altar era evidentemente um quadrado oco ( **27.1** , **8** ), com a grade colocada dentro dele para permitir que as cinzas caíssem no chão abaixo.

27:6 And thou shalt make staves for the altar, staves of shittim wood,.... Like those that were made for the ark, and for the same purpose: and overlay them with brass; with plates of brass, whereas those for the ark were overlaid with gold.

27:7 And the staves shall be put into the rings,.... Not into the rings of the grate, as Jarchi and others: though Dr. Lightfoot (f) thinks these came out of each corner through the altar frame, and hung out of the frame, and in these the staves being put, made the frame and the grate sure together, and so they were also carried together; but it seems rather, that as the grate had rings peculiar to that, to let it down and take it up, and with which it was carried, with a purple cloth covered over it, Num 4:13 so the altar had rings peculiar to that on the sides of it, into which these staves were put: and the staves shall be upon the two sides of the altar,…

27:8 Hollow with boards - It seems to have been a kind of frame-work, and to have had nothing solid in the inside, and only covered with the grating at the top. This rendered it more light and portable.

O planejamento para o pátio previa paredes de cortinas feitas de linho finamente tecido penduradas em postes com bases de bronze. Os postes parecem ter sido espaçados a 2,28 metros e com 2,28 metros de altura. As cortinas formavam um retângulo de 45,72 metros de comprimento e 22,86 metros de largura, com uma abertura de 9,14 metros centrada ao leste.\n\n

A cortina que protegia a entrada do pátio era semelhante em cor e acabamento à cortina de entrada e à cortina divisória do Tabernáculo ( **26.31** , **36** ).

27:17 All the pillars round about the court shall be filleted with silver,.... This is observed, because only mention is made before of the pillars that were on the south and north sides of the court, as filleted with silver; but inasmuch as those at both ends, east and west, were to be so likewise, this is added: their hooks shall be of silver, and their sockets of brass; no notice having been taken of the hooks to the pillars at both ends, though they were as necessary there as elsewhere, and must be supposed, and though the sockets are mentioned, yet not their metal, and therefore are in general included here.

27:18 The length of the court shall be one hundred cubits,.... And as may be concluded from the length of the hangings on each side: and the breadth fifty everywhere; at both ends, and was the breadth of the hangings there, and which all around made the court: and the height five cubits; or two yards and a half, and somewhat more; it was but half the height of the tabernacle, and hence that might be seen above it every way;
O uso de estacas de barraca sugere que os postes do pátio não eram autossustentáveis, mas eram estabilizados com cabos de sustentação.

A primeira coisa dita sobre a função dos sacerdotes, Arão e seus filhos , é que eles deveriam manter as lâmpadas acesas continuamente durante a noite (veja **30.8** ). À medida que as lâmpadas queimavam na presença do Senhor , elas indicariam a continuidade de sua presença.

o Tabernáculo (literalmente a Tenda da reunião ou encontro ): A expressão Tenda do encontro às vezes se refere à tenda do Tabernáculo; nesses casos, a expressão é essencialmente sinônima de Tabernáculo, e a NVT consistentemente a traduz como Tabernáculo . Além disso, uma tenda temporária também chamada de “Tenda da reunião” é mencionada em **33.7-11** . Veja nota de estudo em 40.2 .
